description_long: This object is a short sword or large dagger with a straight, double-edged blade that tapers to a sharp point. The blade is dark, likely made of metal, and appears robust and functional. The hilt features a prominent crossguard with a slightly curved design, finished in a brass or bronze tone. The handle is cylindrical, wrapped in a reddish-brown material, possibly leather, with visible ridges for improved grip and ergonomics. The pommel is rounded and metallic, matching the crossguard, and adds balance to the weapon. There are no visible engravings or decorative elements, giving the sword a utilitarian and classic appearance.
description: A straight, double-edged short sword with a dark blade, brass-toned crossguard, and a ridged, leather-wrapped handle. The design is functional and classic, with no visible ornamentation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the dark blade, the brass-toned crossguard, and the ridged, leather-wrapped handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the blade and the rounded pommel, with the crossguard appearing as a narrow bar.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, showing the blade, crossguard, and handle from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thinness of the blade and the cylindrical handle, similar to view 1.
attribute_handle_design: cylindrical, leather-wrapped with ridges, rounded metallic pommel
